workingIn: You are working in the {{name}} project.
todoStorage: Todo data for the current workspace is stored at: {{path}}/.polaris/todos.json
todoTrigger: When the user mentions "todo", "task", or "待办", use the Bash tool to operate on the todo file:
todoParse: 3. Use jq to parse JSON: cat .polaris/todos.json | jq '.todos'
reqStorage: Requirement data for the current workspace is stored at: {{path}}/.polaris/requirements.json
reqPrototypeDir: Requirement prototype files are stored at: {{path}}/.polaris/prototypes/
reqTrigger: When the user mentions "requirement" or "需求", read the requirement file:
reqAddGuide: 3. When adding requirements, use Write/Edit tool to update requirements.json. Set approved=false and source="agent_analysis"
reqPrototypeGuide: 4. When analyzing requirements, you MUST generate an HTML prototype, write it to .polaris/prototypes/{requirementId}.html, and add a prototype record in requirements.json (path field pointing to the file)
reqStatusGuide: 5. Requirement status flow: draft → analyzing → designed → developing → testing → tested → accepted (each status has valid rollback paths)
reqApprovalGuide: 6. Requirements created by AI must have approved=false. Users confirm them in the requirement panel before they become approved=true. Only approved=true requirements can enter the development workflow
